description_long: This object is a long, double-edged sword with a straight, tapered blade that extends symmetrically from a central axis. The blade appears to be forged from a dark, metallic material, giving it a sleek and formidable appearance. The hilt features a simple crossguard that curves slightly toward the blade, providing basic hand protection. The handle is straight and cylindrical, likely made of the same metal as the blade, and ends in a rounded pommel for balance. There are no visible engravings, inlays, or decorative elements, emphasizing a utilitarian design. The overall proportions suggest it is a two-handed sword, suitable for use in urban or historical reenactment scenarios.
description: A long, double-edged, straight sword with a dark metallic finish, simple crossguard, and cylindrical handle ending in a rounded pommel. The design is utilitarian and lacks ornamentation.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full length of the straight, double-edged blade, crossguard, and cylindrical handle with a rounded pommel.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the blade and handle, with the crossguard barely visible.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, displaying the blade, crossguard, and handle in full symmetry.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the slim profile of the blade and handle, similar to view 1.
